subject: [PATCH v3 00/20] iomap: convert to in-iter iomap_next() model

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the iomap_iter() function's return semantics, acknowledged that it needs to zero iter.status on error paths, and confirmed no functional change is intended.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the interaction between ->iomap_next() and ->iomap_end(), explaining that iomap_iter() uses ->iomap_next() when available, but falls back to ->iomap_begin()/->iomap_end() if not. The author did not indicate any plans for a fix.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author Fengnan Chang pointed out that the ->iomap_next() model causes an extra indirect call and iomap_iter() frames on every I/O for DIO reads on filesystems without a ->iomap_end() work, such as ext4. Author Joanne Koong responded by adding a new flag IOMAP_DIO_NO_IOMAP_END to allow callers to promise there is no ->iomap_end() work and no folio batch, letting iomap_dio_simple() skip the finishing iomap_iter() on both success and error-exit paths.
sentiment_signals: added new code to address issue

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the xfs iomap_ops implementation, specifically that it was not using the new ->iomap_next() callback as intended. The author explained that each callback is generated with macros that wrap the iomap_iter_next() helper to finish the previous mapping if needed and produce the next one.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the conversion of btrfs to the new ->iomap_next() callback, explaining that it is generated using the DEFINE_IOMAP_ITER_NEXT_END() macro and does not introduce functional changes.
reviews.raw_body:
Convert btrfs iomap_ops to the new ->iomap_next() callback. The callback is
generated with the DEFINE_IOMAP_ITER_NEXT_END() macro, which wraps the
iomap_iter_next() helper to finish the previous mapping if needed and
produce the next one. No functional changes are intended.

Signed-off-by: Joanne Koong <joannelkoong@gmail.com>
---
 fs/btrfs/direct-io.c | 6 ++++--
 1 file changed, 4 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/fs/btrfs/direct-io.c b/fs/btrfs/direct-io.c
index 460326d34143..d5439b06cdc9 100644
--- a/fs/btrfs/direct-io.c
+++ b/fs/btrfs/direct-io.c
@@ -798,9 +798,11 @@ static void btrfs_dio_submit_io(const struct iomap_iter *iter, struct bio *bio,
 	btrfs_submit_bbio(bbio, 0);
 }
 
+static DEFINE_IOMAP_ITER_NEXT_END(btrfs_dio_iomap_next, btrfs_dio_iomap_begin,
+				  btrfs_dio_iomap_end);
+
 static const struct iomap_ops btrfs_dio_iomap_ops = {
-	.iomap_begin            = btrfs_dio_iomap_begin,
-	.iomap_end              = btrfs_dio_iomap_end,
+	.iomap_next             = btrfs_dio_iomap_next,
 };
 
 static const struct iomap_dio_ops btrfs_dio_ops = {
-- 
2.52.0

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the conversion of ntfs3 to the new ->iomap_next() callback model, explaining that it is generated using the DEFINE_IOMAP_ITER_NEXT_END() macro and does not introduce any functional changes.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the conversion of the ntfs filesystem's iomap_ops to use the new ->iomap_next() callback, explaining that each callback is generated using the DEFINE_IOMAP_ITER_NEXT()/DEFINE_IOMAP_ITER_NEXT_END() macros and no functional changes are intended.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the conversion of ext4 iomap_ops to the new ->iomap_next() callback, explaining that each callback is generated using the DEFINE_IOMAP_ITER_NEXT() macro and no functional changes are intended.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the conversion of erofs iomap_ops to the new ->iomap_next() callback, explaining that each callback is generated using macros and no functional changes are intended.
reviews.raw_body:
Convert erofs iomap_ops to the new ->iomap_next() callback. Each
callback is generated with the DEFINE_IOMAP_ITER_NEXT() and
DEFINE_IOMAP_ITER_NEXT_END() macros, which wrap the iomap_iter_next()
helper to finish the previous mapping if needed and produce the next
one. No functional changes are intended.

Reviewed-by: Gao Xiang <hsiangkao@linux.alibaba.com>
Signed-off-by: Joanne Koong <joannelkoong@gmail.com>
---
 fs/erofs/data.c | 6 ++++--
 fs/erofs/zmap.c | 5 ++++-
 2 files changed, 8 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

diff --git a/fs/erofs/data.c b/fs/erofs/data.c
index 9aa48c8d67d1..d2f01245ee79 100644
--- a/fs/erofs/data.c
+++ b/fs/erofs/data.c
@@ -380,9 +380,11 @@ static int erofs_iomap_end(struct inode *inode, loff_t pos, loff_t length,
 	return written;
 }
 
+static DEFINE_IOMAP_ITER_NEXT_END(erofs_iomap_next, erofs_iomap_begin,
+				  erofs_iomap_end);
+
 static const struct iomap_ops erofs_iomap_ops = {
-	.iomap_begin = erofs_iomap_begin,
-	.iomap_end = erofs_iomap_end,
+	.iomap_next = erofs_iomap_next,
 };
 
 int erofs_fiemap(struct inode *inode, struct fiemap_extent_info *fieinfo,
diff --git a/fs/erofs/zmap.c b/fs/erofs/zmap.c
index bab521613552..3966b62a7051 100644
--- a/fs/erofs/zmap.c
+++ b/fs/erofs/zmap.c
@@ -821,6 +821,9 @@ static int z_erofs_iomap_begin_report(struct inode *inode, loff_t offset,
 	return 0;
 }
 
+static DEFINE_IOMAP_ITER_NEXT(z_erofs_iomap_next_report,
+			      z_erofs_iomap_begin_report);
+
 const struct iomap_ops z_erofs_iomap_report_ops = {
-	.iomap_begin = z_erofs_iomap_begin_report,
+	.iomap_next = z_erofs_iomap_next_report,
 };
-- 
2.52.0

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch series converts the iomap code to use an iterator model, replacing the current begin/end pair of callbacks with a single next callback. This change allows for better performance by enabling inlining and devirtualization of the iteration loop, as well as providing more flexibility for future callers to express custom logic in the IO path. The patches are broken down into 20 parts, including refactoring existing code, converting each filesystem, removing legacy paths, and updating documentation.

2026-07-21.patch_summary: This patch series converts the iomap code to use an iterator model, replacing the current begin/end pair of callbacks with a single next callback. This change allows for better performance by enabling inlining and devirtualization of the iteration loop, as well as providing more flexibility for future callers to express custom logic in the IO path. The patches are broken down into 20 parts, including refactoring existing code, converting each filesystem, removing legacy paths, and updating documentation.
